design_signals: Oversized athletic photography and dominant imagery
Bold black-and-white contrast with a single urgent accent color
Short imperative headline copy — 2 to 5 words
Minimal UI chrome — content and product lead
design_lesson: When the product promise is energy or transformation, scale and contrast should lead. Let imagery and typography do the persuasion before any body copy is read.
type_direction: Oversized bold condensed or expanded headline. Minimal body copy. Single CTA stands alone.
color_direction: Black-and-white primary palette. One vivid accent for urgency. High contrast always.

design_signals: Generous white space as the primary design element
Precise product framing — one product, clean background
Restrained color — neutral surfaces, single accent used sparingly
Controlled motion — short, purposeful, never gratuitous
emotional_effect: Calm confidence, quality, precision, premium trust
design_lesson: When the product promise is quality or craftsmanship, remove clutter and let material and spacing do the persuasion. Restraint is the signal.
type_direction: Clean sans-serif at scale. Short declarative headlines. Generous line-height. Nothing competes with the product.
color_direction: White or near-white surfaces. Deep neutral text. One brand accent used at peak moments only.

design_signals: Familiar blue-white foundation — jakob's law in action
Repetitive interaction patterns — avatars, reactions, chips, modals
Rounded surfaces and consistent density
Constant engagement cues close to content
emotional_effect: Familiarity, continuity, low learning cost, social immediacy
design_lesson: Very large ecosystems need repeated signals and familiar patterns more than novelty. Innovation should happen in value, not in basic comprehension.
type_direction: System font or high-legibility neutral sans. Consistent hierarchy across all surfaces. No decorative type.
color_direction: Blue-white core. High-signal accent for primary action. Semantic colors for status and interaction states.

design_signals: Dark surfaces that create immersion and focus
Vivid accents — especially brand green for action
Bold expressive cover art as the primary visual content
Strong typographic rhythm across playlist and library layouts
emotional_effect: Energy, immersion, personal identity, taste, presence
design_lesson: When content is expressive, the chrome should support mood without fighting the content. Dark surfaces let content breathe. High-saturation accents create energy without overwhelming.
type_direction: Bold geometric sans for headlines. Consistent utility sans for metadata. High contrast on dark surfaces.
color_direction: Dark background with vivid accent. Brand green as the single action color. Let content imagery provide color variety.

design_signals: Warm human photography — people and spaces, not products
Soft curves and rounded components
Human-centered copy — emotional but plain
High trust spacing — nothing feels rushed or compressed
design_lesson: Hospitality and marketplace products must reduce risk perception and increase warmth simultaneously. Photography should tell the human story; UI should step back and support.
type_direction: Friendly but professional sans-serif. Warm body copy. Headline that speaks to the experience, not just the transaction.
color_direction: Warm neutral backgrounds. Brand coral or red accent used sparingly. Photography provides color richness.

design_signals: Code-forward visuals — documentation and product integrated
Strong typographic hierarchy with disciplined spacing
Confident minimalism — no decoration that doesn't serve communication
Precise grid and alignment — everything has a reason
design_lesson: Technical products win when clarity and polish coexist. Let the product's capability speak; the interface should feel like a craftsperson built it.
type_direction: Precise neutral sans for UI. Code font for technical content. Strong H1–H3 hierarchy. Generous body line-height.
color_direction: Off-white or light gray surfaces. Deep neutral text. Indigo or purple accent. Semantic green and red for status.
